SEARCH
Package sttrj.game

Class Quadrant

Object
Quadrant
public class Quadrant extends Object
Quadrant are sections of space with a randomised mixture of Stars, Klingons and Starbases.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Quadrant(int galaxyX, int galaxyY)
    Constructs a Quadrant at the given coordinates.
    Quadrant(int galaxyX, int galaxyY, int starbases, int klingons, int stars)
    Overloaded constructor to instantiate a Quadrant with a given number of objects set.

  • Constructor Details

    • Quadrant

      public Quadrant(int galaxyX, int galaxyY)
      Constructs a Quadrant at the given coordinates.
      Parameters:
      galaxyX - horizontal coordinate for this Quadrant in the Galaxy.
      galaxyY - vertical coordinate for this Quadrant in the Galaxy.

    • Quadrant

      public Quadrant(int galaxyX, int galaxyY, int starbases, int klingons, int stars)
      Overloaded constructor to instantiate a Quadrant with a given number of objects set. Can specify a given number of Starbases, Klingons, and Stars to be generated in a given Quadrant Originally used only for testing, so it had a very specific order for testing, however this constructor was repurposed to enable Starbases to be replenished with energy for starships (and also allows things to move about in the Quadrant between visits. Use this constructor (without randomisation) for your own testing, while developing the game However, once you have it working, you need to modify the code to put everything in random positions in the Quadrant -- otherwise, all the Starbases, Klingons and Stars are going to be on the top row from the top left corner.

      PS As a reminder, only one Starbase, Star or Klingon should be at a specific x,y coordinate for the Quadrant, they should never overlap.

      Parameters:
      galaxyX - horizontal coordinate
      galaxyY - vertical coordinate
      starbases - number of Starbase for the Quadrant to place on empty sectors in this Quadrant
      klingons - number of Klingon for the Quadrant to place on empty sectors in this Quadrant
      stars - number of Star for the Quadrant to place on empty sectors in this Quadrant

  • Method Details

    • klingonCount

      public int klingonCount()
      Returns the number of live Klingons in this Quadrant.
      Returns:
      the number of live Klingons in this Quadrant.

    • starbaseCount

      public int starbaseCount()
      Returns the number of Starbase in this Quadrant.
      Returns:
      the number of Starbase in this Quadrant.

    • starCount

      public int starCount()
      Returns the number of Star in this Quadrant.
      Returns:
      the number of Star in this Quadrant.

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Returns the x-coordinate for this Quadrant.
      Returns:
      the x-coordinate for this Quadrant.

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Returns the y-coordinate for this Quadrant.
      Returns:
      the y-coordinate for this Quadrant.

    • getSymbolAt

      public String getSymbolAt(int x, int y)
      Returns the 3 character String symbol at the given x,y coordinates checking the Stars, Klingons, and Starbases for any of those at the given coordinates, if none are returns " ".
      Parameters:
      x - horizontal coordinate.
      y - vertical coordinate.
      Returns:
      3 character String symbol.

    • getEntityAt

      public Entity getEntityAt(int x, int y)
      Searches the Quadrant for any Entitys at the given location.
      Parameters:
      x - horizontal coordinate
      y - vertical coordinate
      Returns:
      if there is an Entity at the given x, y coordinate in the Quadrant return that, otherwise return null.

    • klingonsMarkedForRemovalCount

      public int klingonsMarkedForRemovalCount()
      Returns how many Klingons are currently marked for removal.
      Returns:
      how many Klingons are currently marked for removal.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a descriptive String representation of this Quadrant's state.
      • the first line provides this Quadrant's position Quadrant(x,y)
      • the second line provides how many Stars there are Stars: n
      • the third line provides how many Klingons there are Enemies: n
      • the fourth line provides how many Starbases there are Starbases: n
      The final line includes a trailing newline character.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      the 4-line descriptive String representation of this Quadrant

    • scan

      public void scan()
      Scan every Klingon, Starbase and Star in this Quadrant. Slightly redundant because we always set Stars as already scanned, but scan them again in case something went wrong.

    • symbol

      public String symbol()
      Returns a String representation of the Quadrant formatted as DIGITDIGITDIGIT i.e. 123
      • the first number is how many Stars there are in this Quadrant.
      • the second number is how many Starbases there are in this Quadrant.
      • the third number is how many Klingons there are in this Quadrant.

      So a Quadrant with 2 Stars, 0 Starbases and 3 Klingons would have a String representation of 203.

      Returns:
      a String representation of the Quadrant

    • tick

      public void tick(Game game)
      Handles a 'turn' of the game for the Starbases and Klingons in this Quadrant. Intended to be called only if the Quadrant is currently active. Attempts to replenish Enterprise if docked at a Starbase. Deals with attacks from any Klingons if not docked at a Starbase.
      Parameters:
      game - the current Game state for us to manipulate.

    • hit

      public void hit(int damage)
      Hit every Klingon in this Quadrant.
      Parameters:
      damage - how much damage to do to EACH Klingon.

    • getRandomEmptySector

      public XyPair getRandomEmptySector()
      Returns a random empty sector position as an XyPair.
      Returns:
      the position of an empty sector position represented as a XyPair.

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup()
      Removes any Klingon and Starbase that have been marked for removal from their respective List. Only public for testing purposes.
